title: Apnea Bradycardia Hypoxemia and the Foundation of Respiratory Control

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Apnea, Bradycardia, and Respiratory Control?
Apnea ไม่ใช่แค่ monitor alarm — มันคือ window เข้าไปใน brainstem maturation ของทารก. (1) Apnea of prematurity เกิดในทารกเกือบทุกรายที่ GA <28 wk หรือ BW <1,000 g — เข้าใจ mechanism → รักษาถูกวิธี; (2) Caffeine (CAP trial) ลด BPD, ↑ survival without NDI, ลด PDA, ลด severe ROP — drug ตัวเดียวที่ทำได้ทุกอย่างนี้ เพราะทำงานผ่าน adenosine receptor blockade; (3) Intermittent hypoxemia (IH) ↔ ROP, BPD, neurodevelopmental impairment — ไม่ใช่แค่ desaturation แต่เป็น oxidative stress cascade; (4) GER ≠ cause of apnea ในส่วนใหญ่ของ preterm — ข้อมูลจาก impedance-pH probe แสดงว่าไม่มี temporal link → antacid therapy เพิ่ม NEC risk; (5) SIDS link: 5-HT transporter polymorphism → ↓ arousal → ↑ SIDS risk. ความรู้ในบทนี้ = ชีวิตทารกทุกคนใน NICU.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย fetal breathing movement (FBM): timing, function, pattern เปลี่ยนแปลงจาก early gestation ถึง term, และ response ต่อ hypoxemia vs hypercapnia ใน utero
อธิบาย postnatal maturation ของ respiratory control: irregular breathing pattern, biphasic hypoxic ventilatory response, laryngeal chemoreflex (LCR), Hering-Breuer reflex
ระบุ roles ของ GABA, adenosine, endorphins, prostaglandins, สรุป serotonin ใน respiratory control และ apnea pathogenesis
จำแนก types of apnea: central, obstructive, mixed — ระบุ most common type ใน preterm, monitoring limitations
ให้ CHIME definition ของ bradycardia; ให้ common SpO2 thresholds ของ IH; ระบุ IH consequences (ROP, BPD, NDI)
อธิบาย pathogenesis ของ apnea of prematurity: central chemosensitivity, peripheral chemoreceptor (↑ หรือ ↓), LCR, apneic threshold CO2, REM sleep
ระบุ clinical associations ของ apnea: sepsis (IL-1 → PGE2 pathway), anemia (↓ O2 delivery), BPD, metabolic causes, medications
อธิบาย GER-apnea relationship: ไม่มี evidence ว่า GER causes apnea ในส่วนใหญ่; ranitidine/PPI → ↑ NEC risk
รู้ BRUE: definition, low-risk vs high-risk criteria (2016 AAP), แทน ALTE
อธิบาย treatment: CPAP mechanism, caffeine pharmacology (CAP trial: dose, benefits), doxapram (ไม่ recommend), prone positioning, SpO2 target range
ระบุ timeline resolution ของ apnea of prematurity; 7-day event-free interval → 95% success; discharge criteria (AAP 2016)
อธิบาย apnea of prematurity vs SIDS: timeline relationship; 5-HTT polymorphism; Back to Sleep ↓ SIDS >50%
1. Fetal Breathing Movement (FBM)
1.1 ลักษณะและ Function
Onset: detectable by ultrasound from 11 weeks' gestation — เกิดใน many species; เกิดก่อน placenta becomes sole gas exchanger
Function ของ FBM: (1) lung growth and development (↓ diaphragmatic activity → pulmonary hypoplasia); (2) ↑ fetal cardiac output; (3) ↑ blood flow to heart, brain, placenta

Pattern evolution: early gestation: continuous movement (originates from spinal cord) → 3rd trimester: phasic pattern during REM sleep only; complete cessation during non-REM — secondary to descending inhibitory pontine input to medullary rhythm-generating center
Mediators ของ FBM transition: serotonin, GABA, corticotrophin-releasing factor, prostaglandins
1.2 Chemoreceptor Responses ใน Utero

Hypercapnia → ↑ FBM: increase in incidence + depth of FBM during REM sleep only; present from 24 wk GA; CO2 sensitivity ↑ with advancing GA. Fetal hypocapnia → cessation of FBM — baseline CO2 is required for FBM to exist
Fetal PaO2: 23–27 mmHg (relative hypoxemia vs adult); O2 delivery adequate matching consumption; UNLIKE adults: fetus DECREASES FBM in response to hypoxemia
Hypoxic depression mechanism: central origin — descending pontine/suprapontine inhibition (brainstem transection → hypoxemia stimulates FBM even without carotid bodies)
Physiologic logic: ↑ breathing during fetal hypoxemia would be counterproductive — placenta = gas exchanger, not lungs
2. Postnatal Maturation ของ Respiratory Control
2.1 Neonatal Breathing Pattern
Irregular breathing: spontaneous cycling between eupnea, apnea, periodic breathing, tachypnea — respiratory frequency inversely proportional to body weight
TE prolongation → apnea: periods of slow rate = prolonged expiratory time (TE); inspiratory time (TI) relatively unchanged during development; extreme TE prolongation = respiratory pauses and apnea
Paradoxical rib cage movement: highly compliant rib cage + ↓ intercostal tone + diaphragmatic contraction → inward movement on inspiration → ↑↑ work of breathing
Compensation: shortened TE + expiratory braking to maintain FRC; sighs reopen atelectasis; apnea → ↓ FRC → ↓ O2 stores
Epidemiology: apnea ≈ 100% of infants <28 wk or BW <1000 g; resolves to term-neonate baseline by 43–44 wk PCA; even healthy term infants may have apnea >20 sec on home monitoring
2.2 Biphasic Hypoxic Ventilatory Response (HVR)

Biphasic HVR — ความแตกต่างจาก Adult
Adult: sustained ventilatory response to hypoxemia. Neonate: (1) initial ↑ ventilation (1–2 min) via peripheral chemoreceptor (carotid body) activation — eliminated by carotid body denervation; (2) late decline → BELOW baseline (hypoxic ventilatory depression, HVD) — central origin: adenosine, GABA, endorphins, descending inhibitory pontine tracts. Tidal volume stays elevated but frequency ↓ → biphasic appearance. HVD ↑ with ↓ GA and ↓ postnatal age. IMPORTANT: hypoxia → flat CO2 response curve (Fig 68.2) — the more hypoxic the infant, the flatter the CO2 response.
2.3 Hypercapnia and CO2 Response
Central chemoreceptors: retrotrapezoid nucleus, NTS, locus coeruleus, rostral VRG, medullary raphe — NOT confined to ventrolateral medulla only
Peripheral chemoreceptors (carotid body): contribute 10–40% of total hypercapnic response
Serotonergic (5-HT) neurons: important for ventilatory control + arousal to hypercapnia; perinatal nicotine exposure → ↓ chemosensitivity of 5-HT neurons (Avraam et al Neuroscience 2020)

Preterm CO2 response: impaired vs term/adults; more impaired in preterm WITH apnea vs without; ↑ with advancing GA + postnatal age. Apneic threshold: baseline PaCO2 is only 1–1.3 mmHg above the apneic threshold → any small drop → apnea (Khan et al J Appl Physiol 2005;98:1171)
2.4 Laryngeal Chemoreflex (LCR)
Trigger: chemical (water, NH4Cl, acid) or mechanical stimulation of laryngeal mucosa → inhibition of breathing + glottic closure + swallowing + bradycardia + hypotension
Preterm exaggeration: prolonged apnea response to saline in oropharynx; matures with advancing GA; similar maturation profile to apnea of prematurity

Mediators: adenosine A2A agonist → ↑ LCR; GABAA blockade → prevent LCR; adenosine + GABA pathway interact — A2A receptors co-localize on GABAergic neurons in medulla (Abu-Shaweesh J Appl Physiol 2007)
Hering-Breuer reflex: ↑ lung volume → ↓ TI + ↑ TE; activated by CPAP — prevents overdistension; deflation reflex: ↑ TI in term; preterm → paradoxical: ↑ respiratory pauses → ↓ recovery from apnea
3. Neurotransmitters ใน Respiratory Control

rows: Major inhibitory NT; GABAA agonist → ↓ ventilation
↑ GABAA receptor density + different subunit mix in preterm brainstem; may switch excitatory→inhibitory at birth; mediates HVD + LCR + ↓ CO2 response
Inhibits neural function + respiration via A1 receptors; ↑ with hypoxemia
Basis of methylxanthine therapy: caffeine blocks A1 + A2A → ↑ neuronal activity, ↑ NE/dopamine/serotonin; A2A on GABAergic neurons → less GABA release → less inhibition
Naloxone → improves apnea/periodic breathing in infants with elevated CSF β-endorphin (blocks endorphin-mediated respiratory depression); opiates/benzos as NICU drugs → cause apnea
PGE1 infusion for ductus-dependent CHD → respiratory depression; indomethacin → ↑ phrenic activity (reverses PGE1 effect)
Complex: both activation + inhibition depending on receptor subtype
5-HT1A blockade reverses apneustic breathing; raphe 5-HT neurons → maturation of central chemoreception; 5-HTT long allele → ↑ SIDS risk

blocks.text: 4. Definitions — Apnea, Bradycardia, Intermittent Hypoxemia
4.1 Types of Apnea

Apnea Classification
Definition: cessation of breathing >15–20 sec; shorter events (<15 sec) qualify IF + SpO2 desaturation + bradycardia. Central: total cessation of respiratory effort, no obstruction. Obstructive: respiratory effort against obstructed upper airway → chest wall motion without air flow; pharynx = most common site. Mixed: obstructed effort FOLLOWING central pause — most common type in preterm infants. Forms a continuum. MONITORING GAP: impedance monitoring detects central apnea + bradycardia ONLY — completely MISSES obstructive + mixed apnea. Nursing documentation also unreliable + does not correlate with electronic recordings.
4.2 Bradycardia Definitions
General: HR <100 bpm OR <70–80 bpm in convalescing preterm approaching discharge
CHIME study criteria (<44 wk PMA): HR <60 bpm for ≥5 sec OR <80 bpm for ≥15 sec
Quality improvement target: HR <80 bpm for >5 sec (Coughlin et al Pediatrics 2020;145:e20190861)
No international consensus on clinically significant definition — major implications for NICU policies and LOS
4.3 Intermittent Hypoxemia (IH)
Common thresholds: SpO2 <80%, <85%, or <90% — no standard definition
Consequences: ROP (Raffay et al Pediatr Res 2019;85:318); BPD — Jensen et al AJRCCM 2021;204:1192: IH associated with severe BPD; neurodevelopmental impairment; death; wheezing
Mechanism: oxidative stress from wide SpO2 fluctuations; chronic IH → sympathetic activation → T2DM, HTN, heart failure in animal models; carotid body sensitization from free-radical injury
Low O2 target → ↑ IH: Di Fiore et al J Pediatr 2012;161:1047 — low SpO2 target range → ↑ IH incidence
5. Pathogenesis ของ Apnea of Prematurity
5.1 Mechanisms — Overview
Apnea of prematurity คือ developmental disorder ที่ reflect physiologic immaturity ไม่ใช่ pathologic immaturity — แต่เมื่อรุนแรง อาจเป็น sign of superimposed illness.

rows: ↓ central chemosensitivity (flat CO2 response); HVD; ↑ inhibitory NT (GABA, adenosine); ↓ serotonin
↓ carotid body activity (initial); ↑ carotid body activity (with BPD/repeated IH); LCR exaggeration; excessive bradycardic response
Genetic predisposition (PHOX2B, adenosine receptor A2A polymorphisms); sepsis + cytokines; bilirubin; anemia

blocks.text: 5.2 Apneic Threshold

Critical concept: baseline PaCO2 is only 1–1.3 mmHg above apneic threshold (Khan 2005) → any small CO2 drop (from mild hyperventilation, startle, stimulation) → apnea; hypoxemia → ↓ metabolism → ↓ CO2 → narrow gap further
REM sleep: predominant in preterm; primary mechanism = loss of upper airway muscle tone → pharyngeal collapse → obstructive component; paradoxical breathing during REM → ↑ HVD

Bradycardia mechanism: (1) hypoxic stimulation of carotid body chemoreceptors in absence of lung inflation; (2) simultaneous activation of laryngeal receptors → central mechanism; exact pathway still under investigation
5.3 Carotid Body — Enhanced vs Reduced Sensitivity
In utero: carotid chemo O2 sensitivity adapted to fetal PaO2 23–27 mmHg; at birth: fourfold ↑ PaO2 → chemoreceptors silenced; gradual maturation of hypoxic chemosensitivity follows
Reduced sensitivity: contributes to apnea of prematurity; apnea persists many weeks despite carotid body maturation

Enhanced sensitivity (BPD infants): repeated IH → conditioning → facilitation of carotid body discharge → destabilizes breathing; preterm with high apnea frequency found to have ↑ carotid body activity (Nock et al J Pediatr 2004)
6. Clinical Associations
6.1 Sepsis and Inflammation

Mechanism: systemic IL-1 → binds IL-1R on blood-brain barrier endothelium → induces PGE2 synthesis → PGE2 released into respiratory brainstem regions → respiratory depression (Hofstetter PNAS 2007;104:9894)
Intrapulmonary inflammation: ↑ brainstem cytokine expression + ↓ HVR (Kannan Resp Physiol Neurobiol 2011;178:458)
New-onset or escalating apnea → ALWAYS investigate for sepsis/NEC; older infant: RSV/viral = apnea herald
6.2 Anemia
Mechanism: ↓ O2-carrying capacity → ↑ hypoxic respiratory depression; pRBC transfusion → ↓ computer-detected apneic events + ↓ IH frequency
Zagol et al J Pediatr 2012;161:417: inverse relationship between admission hematocrit and probability of future apnea; ↓ hematocrit = major trigger
6.3 GER — Not Proven Cause

GER ≠ Cause of Apnea ใน Preterm ส่วนใหญ่
Evidence: impedance-pH probe studies: temporal association between GER + apnea extremely rare; when they coincide, GER does not prolong or worsen apnea severity (Di Fiore J Perinatol 2010;30:683). Antacid/PPI therapy: NOT benign — H2 blockers + PPI → ↑ infection + NEC. Ranitidine (Terrin et al Pediatrics 2012;129:e40): ranitidine-exposed VLBW infants had significant ↑ in infections, NEC, and death. Exception: small subset (~3/7 in one study) had true temporal link; fundoplication helped in 2/3 (Nunez J Pediatr Gastroenterol Nutr 2011;52:523) — but this is NOT routine. สรุป: do NOT treat apnea with antacids routinely.
6.4 Other Associations
Metabolic: hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia, electrolyte imbalances, temperature instability, inborn errors of metabolism
Medications: opiates, benzodiazepines, magnesium sulfate, PGE1 → all cause apnea
BPD: ~50% of extremely preterm have BPD; immature respiratory control + abnormal lung + pulmonary vasoconstriction → prolonged apnea course

Periodic breathing: short breath cycles alternating with pauses; absent first few days; ↑ frequency at 2–4 wk; persists in term infants up to 6 months; acute ↑ periodic breathing = illness signal (NEC, infection in 45% with extreme periodic breathing)
7. BRUE — Brief Resolved Unexplained Events

BRUE (2016 AAP) — แทน ALTE
Definition: event in infant <1 year with ≥1 of: (1) cyanosis/pallor; (2) absent/decreased/irregular breathing; (3) marked change in tone; (4) altered level of responsiveness — no other explanation after full H+P. Low-risk criteria (ALL must be met): >60 days of age; GA ≥32 wk + PCA ≥45 wk; first BRUE; duration <1 min; no CPR by trained provider; benign H+P. High-risk: any of the 4 defining presentations + does NOT meet all low-risk criteria → hospitalization generally needed. Outcome: multicenter retrospective: no diagnosis in most cases; when found, majority benign/self-limited (Bochner et al Pediatrics 2021;148:e2021052673).
8. Treatment
8.1 Noninvasive Respiratory Support

CPAP 4–6 cmH2O: (1) splints upper airway → ↓ pharyngeal/laryngeal collapse (obstructive component); (2) ↑ FRC → delays time to hypoxemia + bradycardia; (3) activates Hering-Breuer → prevents overdistension
BiPAP / NIPPV: 2016 AAP: no evidence to support NIPPV or BiPAP for apnea of prematurity

HFNC: >1 L/min; more comfortable interface; AAP 2016: safety concerns persist for HFNC; consensus guidelines (Yoder et al J Perinatol 2017;37:809): adequate humidification, prong sizing to prevent nare occlusion, flow 4–6 L/min, max 8 L/min
NIV-NAVA: latest; retrospective data: NIV-NAVA vs nonsynchronized NIPPV → ↓ bradycardia events/day (Tabacaru et al Pediatr Pulmonol 2019;54:1742)
8.2 Caffeine — Mechanism and CAP Trial

Caffeine Therapy — CAP Trial (Schmidt et al NEJM 2006; JAMA 2012; JAMA Pediatr 2017)
Mechanism: non-specific adenosine receptor antagonist (A1 + A2A) → ↑ neuronal activity → ↑ NE/dopamine/serotonin release; ↑ minute ventilation; ↑ CO2 sensitivity; ↓ HVD; ↑ diaphragmatic activity; ↓ periodic breathing; direct anti-inflammatory effect on lung (Weichelt Eur Respir J 2013). CAP Trial Results: loading dose 20 mg/kg caffeine citrate, maintenance 5–10 mg/kg/d: (1) ↓ BPD rate significantly; (2) ↑ survival without NDI; (3) ↓ hemodynamically significant PDA; (4) ↓ severe ROP; (5) at 11-year follow-up: no adverse effects on academic performance, motor function, behavior (Schmidt JAMA Pediatr 2017). Adverse effects: tachycardia, dysrhythmias, feeding intolerance, seizures (RARE); ↑ metabolic rate ~20% → ↑ caloric demands; mild diuresis.

rows: Higher doses → stronger respiratory neural output
Variable adenosine receptor inhibition; dose-dependent inflammation; safety concerns; cerebellar injury reported
Available data largely association-based, not RCT
Prolonged therapy → ↓ IH duration until term-adjusted age
Prolongs hospitalization + ↑ home monitor use; lack of strong RCT

blocks.text: 8.3 Other Treatments
Doxapram: stimulates central + peripheral chemoreceptors; NOT recommended — systematic review: limited evidence, adverse effects, no benefit over theophylline (Vliegenthart Neonatology 2017;111:162)

Prone positioning: ↑ cardiorespiratory stability in extremely preterm; position-related ↓ bradycardia/desaturation not affected by PMA (Shepherd et al Sleep 2020;43:zsz256); transition to supine at 32 wk PMA for safe sleep — but prone is clinically superior in the NICU
SpO2 targets: currently 90–95% range; low O2 target → ↑ IH; higher baseline SpO2 → ↓ frequency + severity of desaturation with apnea
Vibratory neuromodulation: limb proprioceptive afferent stimulation (hand + foot vibratory device) → ↓ respiratory pauses, bradycardia, IH (Kesavan et al PLoS One 2016;11:e0157349)
Kangaroo care: widespread acceptance; advocates report ↓ apnea rates; evidence does NOT support ↓ apnea — but proven benefits for stress reduction, bonding, sleep quality
9. Resolution, Consequences, and Discharge
9.1 Timeline of Resolution
Apnea of prematurity: generally resolves by 36–40 wk PCA; returns to term-baseline by 43–44 wk PCA
26-wk GA infants: resolution may take longer; persistent events = frequent bradycardia <70–80 bpm with short pauses rather than prolonged apnea
7-day event-free interval → 95% success rate: Lorch et al Pediatrics 2011;128:e1 — risk of recurrence depends on GA + PMA at last event
9.2 Neurodevelopmental Consequences
Causality difficult: apnea + brain injury coexist in preterm — cannot separate cause + effect

Evidence mounting: (1) delayed apnea resolution + severe apnea course → ↑ risk impaired NDO (Pillekamp Neonatology 2007;91:155); (2) Greene et al J Perinatol 2014;34:562: ↑ bradycardia frequency/severity → worse language development at 1–2 years in ELBW; (3) Poets et al JAMA 2015;314:595 (Canadian Oxygen Trial): prolonged IH episodes in first 2–3 months → adverse 18-month outcomes
Former preterm → ↑ risk sleep-disordered breathing; IH → neuronal plasticity changes in respiratory control
Extended caffeine until term-adjusted age: proposed + under investigation (Rhein et al JAMA Pediatr 2014;168:250; Dobson et al J Perinatol 2017;37:1135)
9.3 Discharge Criteria (AAP 2016)

Hospital Discharge — AAP 2016 Guidance
Key principles: individual NICUs should develop monitoring policies. Caffeine cessation: consider when apnea/bradycardia-free off positive pressure for 5–7 days OR at 33–34 wk PMA (whichever first). Lower HR alarm settings: reasonable in convalescing infants >33–34 wk. Brief isolated bradycardias: spontaneously resolve + feeding events that resolve with interruption → generally need NOT delay discharge. Inconsistent definitions: major impact on LOS — emphasizes need for unit-level policy standardization.
10. Apnea of Prematurity and SIDS
10.1 Relationship
Apnea of prematurity and SIDS are SEPARATE conditions; prematurity is a significant RISK FACTOR for SIDS
Timeline: apnea of prematurity largely resolves by 44 wk PCA → BEFORE the peak incidence of SIDS at all gestational ages — Malloy & Freeman Pediatrics 2000
Back to Sleep campaign: ↓ SIDS incidence by >50%; relative contribution of maternal smoking + socioeconomic factors ↑ → these remain important targets
10.2 Serotonin and SIDS

5-HTT Polymorphism → SIDS Risk
Finding: SIDS victims (Japanese, African-American, White cohorts) had significant positive association with homozygous long allele of 5-HT transporter promoter (5-HTT) — Weese-Mayer et al Am J Med Genet 2003;117A:268. SIDS victims more likely to express long allele AND miss short allele. Mechanism: ↑ 5-HTT expression → ↓ serotonin availability → delay in maturation of serotonergic neurons in arcuate nucleus + respiratory groups → failure of arousal + inadequate respiratory response to life-threatening event. This is likely the underlying mechanism for SIDS in this subset. SUID (2016 AAP): SIDS = subcategory of sudden unexpected infant death (SUID); also includes ill-defined mortality + accidental suffocation/strangulation. Hospital discharge = model safe sleep environment + parent education.
11. NICU Practical — Apnea Management
11.1 Initial Assessment of New/Escalating Apnea
Step 1: RuleOut pathologic causes — sepsis/NEC, IVH/HIE/seizures, hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia, metabolic disorder, anemia, temperature instability, medications (opioids, MgSO4, PGE1)
Step 2: Evaluate type — is monitoring capturing obstructive/mixed? (impedance misses these); polysomnography if persistent events with unclear cause
Step 3: Optimize baseline — ensure SpO2 90–95%, Hct adequate, no infection; prone position in acute NICU care
Step 4: Consider escalating respiratory support — CPAP/HFNC first; NAVA-synchronized NIPPV if available
Step 5: Caffeine — ensure adequate dose (loading 20 mg/kg, maintenance 5–10 mg/kg/d); check timing (early caffeine ↓ BPD)
11.2 Caffeine Monitoring
Monitor: HR (tachycardia = first sign of toxicity), feeding tolerance, diuresis, weight gain, jitteriness
Theophylline: tachycardia, GI intolerance, seizures at toxic levels; level monitoring required (therapeutic: 6–12 μg/mL)
Caffeine: wider therapeutic window; levels generally not needed unless concern for toxicity; therapeutic: 8–20 μg/mL
11.3 GER — What NOT to Do
DO NOT start antacid/H2 blocker/PPI for apnea treatment: evidence base does not support; ranitidine → ↑ NEC + infection + death (Terrin 2012)
Position + feeding adjustments (smaller, more frequent feeds; avoid post-feed agitation) reasonable; treat true symptomatic reflux separately from apnea management
11.4 Discharge Planning
Document event-free interval; 7-day apnea/bradycardia-free = 95% success rate
Educate family on safe sleep environment before discharge (supine position, firm mattress, no co-sleeping, smoke-free environment)
Home cardiorespiratory monitor: NOT routinely indicated; use in specific cases (extreme prematurity, central hypoventilation syndromes) — monitor does NOT prevent SIDS
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Impedance Monitor ≠ Full Apnea Detection
Standard bedside impedance monitoring ตรวจจับ central apnea + bradycardia เท่านั้น — CANNOT detect obstructive or mixed apnea เลย. แต่ mixed apnea คือ most common type ใน preterm! นั่นหมายความว่า apnea burden ที่เห็นบน monitor เป็นแค่ส่วนหนึ่ง. เพราะฉะนั้น: nursing documentation of 'no apnea' ไม่ได้แปลว่าไม่มี apnea จริงๆ — นี่คือสาเหตุที่ monitor data ไม่ correlate กับ electronic recordings. อย่า rely solely on nursing notes สำหรับ apnea assessment.

PEARL 2 — Apneic Threshold ห่างจาก Baseline CO2 แค่ 1–1.3 mmHg
Baseline PaCO2 ของทารกทั้ง preterm และ term ห่างจาก apneic threshold (CO2 level ที่ทำให้ apnea) เพียง 1–1.3 mmHg (Khan J Appl Physiol 2005). ดังนั้น stimulation เล็กน้อย → mild hyperventilation → CO2 drops 1 mmHg → ข้ามผ่าน threshold → apnea. นี่คือเหตุว่าทำไม NGT insertion, heel stick, รถผ่าน, สัมผัสกระทันหัน → triggering apnea ได้ง่ายมากใน preterm. Caffeine → ↑ CO2 sensitivity → ขยาย buffer zone → ↓ apnea risk.

PEARL 3 — CAP Trial: Caffeine ทำได้ 4 อย่างพร้อมกัน
Caffeine (CAP trial, Schmidt et al NEJM 2006): (1) ↓ BPD; (2) ↑ survival without neurodevelopmental impairment; (3) ↓ hemodynamically significant PDA; (4) ↓ severe ROP — จาก drug เดียว. Mechanism: adenosine A1 + A2A blockade → ↑ neuronal activity + ↑ catecholamines + anti-inflammatory (↓ hyperoxia-mediated pulmonary inflammation). Standard dose: loading 20 mg/kg, maintenance 5–10 mg/kg/d. Start early. At 11-year follow-up: no cognitive/behavioral harm (Schmidt JAMA Pediatr 2017).

PEARL 4 — GER ≠ Cause of Apnea: Antacids เพิ่ม NEC
Mitos ที่ต้องทลาย: GER rarely causes apnea ใน preterm infants — temporal link ระหว่าง acid reflux + apnea event แทบไม่มี (Di Fiore 2010; impedance-pH probe study). เมื่อ coincide กัน: GER ไม่ได้ prolong หรือ worsen apnea. ที่สำคัญกว่า: ranitidine ใน VLBW → ↑ infection + NEC + death (Terrin Pediatrics 2012). สรุป: DO NOT treat apnea with antacids — evidence base ไม่รองรับ + มี harm.

PEARL 5 — SIDS + Serotonin: 5-HTT Long Allele → Failure of Arousal
SIDS victims ใน 3 เชื้อชาติมี significant association กับ homozygous long allele ของ 5-HT transporter promoter (5-HTT) — Weese-Mayer Am J Med Genet 2003. Mechanism: ↑ 5-HTT → ↑ serotonin reuptake → ↓ synaptic 5-HT → delay ใน maturation ของ serotonergic neurons ใน arcuate nucleus + respiratory groups → failure of arousal + inadequate respiratory response to life-threatening event. Back to Sleep ↓ SIDS >50% แต่ smoking + socioeconomic factors relative contribution ↑ — ต้อง target กลุ่มนี้.
13. Evidence Update — Key Studies
Schmidt B et al N Engl J Med 2006;354:2112 (CAP trial): caffeine vs placebo in 2006 VLBW infants: ↓ BPD, ↑ survival without NDI; landmark RCT establishing caffeine safety and efficacy
Schmidt B et al JAMA 2012;307:275: CAP 5-year follow-up: ↑ survival without disability — cognitive, motor, behavioral outcomes all better in caffeine group; ↓ PDA and severe ROP confirmed
Schmidt B et al JAMA Pediatr 2017;171:564: CAP 11-year follow-up: no adverse effects on academic performance, motor function, or behavior — long-term safety confirmed
Khan A et al J Appl Physiol 2005;98:1171: measurement of CO2 apneic threshold in newborns: baseline PaCO2 only 1–1.3 mmHg above apneic threshold — mechanistic basis for apnea triggers

Poets CF et al JAMA 2015;314:595 (Canadian Oxygen Trial post-hoc): prolonged IH in first 2–3 months → adverse 18-month NDO in extremely preterm — key evidence linking IH to neurodevelopmental impairment
Lorch SA et al Pediatrics 2011;128:e1: epidemiology of apnea/bradycardia resolution: 7-day event-free interval → 95% success rate; recurrence risk depends on GA + PMA at last event
Terrin G et al Pediatrics 2012;129:e40: ranitidine in VLBW: significant ↑ in infections, NEC, death — evidence against routine antacid therapy
Weese-Mayer DE et al Am J Med Genet 2003;117A:268: 5-HTT long allele association with SIDS in multiple racial cohorts — serotonin-mediated failure of arousal hypothesis
Jensen EA et al AJRCCM 2021;204:1192: IH association with severe BPD in preterm infants — quantified IH burden predicts BPD outcome
Hofstetter AO et al PNAS 2007;104:9894: IL-1 → PGE2 pathway mediates respiratory depression during sepsis — mechanistic basis for apnea as herald of sepsis
Di Fiore JM et al J Pediatr 2012;161:1047: low SpO2 target range → ↑ IH incidence — supports keeping SpO2 90–95% to minimize IH burden
Rhein LM et al JAMA Pediatr 2014;168:250: caffeine decreases IH in preterm infants — RCT supporting extended caffeine use toward term-adjusted age
Zagol K et al J Pediatr 2012;161:417: inverse relationship between hematocrit and apnea probability; pRBC transfusion → ↓ apneic events — anemia as major apnea trigger
Tabacaru CR et al Pediatr Pulmonol 2019;54:1742: NIV-NAVA vs nonsynchronized NIPPV: ↓ bradycardia events/day — NAVA synchronization reduces cardiorespiratory events
Kesavan K et al PLoS One 2016;11:e0157349: limb proprioceptive vibratory stimulation → ↓ apnea, bradycardia, IH — novel neuromodulation approach
Abu-Shaweesh JM J Appl Physiol 2007;103:1205: adenosine A2A receptors on GABAergic neurons in medulla: mechanism linking methylxanthines to LCR prevention
Avraam J et al Neuroscience 2020;446:80: perinatal nicotine → ↓ chemosensitivity of medullary 5-HT neurons after maturation — mechanism for smoking → ↑ SIDS risk

Shepherd KL et al Sleep 2020;43:zsz256: prone positioning improves cardiorespiratory stability in extremely preterm; position effects not related to PMA — supports prone in acute NICU, supine for discharge
14. Self-Check Q&A
Q: อธิบาย biphasic hypoxic ventilatory response ใน neonate และ mechanism ของแต่ละ phase

ตอบ: Phase 1 (1–2 min): ↑ ventilation — via peripheral chemoreceptor activation (carotid body); eliminated by carotid body denervation. Phase 2: late decline BELOW baseline (HVD) — central origin: adenosine, GABA, endorphins, descending inhibitory pontine tracts; tidal volume stays elevated but frequency ↓. UNLIKE adults who have sustained response. HVD ↑ with ↓ GA + ↓ postnatal age. Clinical pearl: hypoxemia → flat CO2 response curve → worse apnea risk.
Q: Types of apnea คืออะไร และ mixed apnea เกิดอย่างไร? Monitoring limitation คืออะไร?

ตอบ: Central: total cessation of respiratory effort. Obstructive: effort against obstructed airway → chest wall motion + no airflow; pharynx = most common site. Mixed: obstructed effort following central pause — most common in preterm. Monitoring gap: impedance monitoring detects ONLY central + bradycardia; completely MISSES obstructive + mixed. Nursing documentation also unreliable — underestimates true burden.
Q: Caffeine ออกฤทธิ์อย่างไร และ CAP trial พิสูจน์อะไรบ้าง?

ตอบ: Mechanism: non-specific adenosine A1 + A2A receptor antagonist → ↑ neuronal activity → ↑ NE/dopamine/serotonin + catecholamines; ↑ minute ventilation, ↑ CO2 sensitivity, ↓ HVD, ↑ diaphragm activity, ↓ periodic breathing, anti-inflammatory. CAP trial: loading 20 mg/kg, maintenance 5–10 mg/kg/d → (1) ↓ BPD; (2) ↑ survival without NDI; (3) ↓ hemodynamically significant PDA; (4) ↓ severe ROP; (5) 11-year f/u: no cognitive/behavioral harm.
Q: Sepsis triggers apnea ผ่าน mechanism ใด?

ตอบ: Pathway: systemic infection → ↑ IL-1 → IL-1 binds IL-1R on blood-brain barrier endothelium → induces PGE2 synthesis → PGE2 released into respiratory-related brainstem regions → respiratory depression (Hofstetter PNAS 2007). Intrapulmonary inflammation → ↑ brainstem cytokines → ↓ HVR. Clinical: new-onset or escalating apnea → ALWAYS work up for sepsis/NEC; older infant: RSV/viral = first sign.
Q: GER เป็น cause ของ apnea ใน preterm หรือไม่? Evidence คืออะไร?

ตอบ: No — NOT a proven cause: impedance-pH probe studies show temporal association between GER + apnea is very rare; when coincide, GER does not prolong or worsen apnea. Risk of treatment: ranitidine in VLBW → ↑ infections, NEC, death (Terrin Pediatrics 2012). Conclusion: DO NOT treat apnea with antacids routinely; only small select subset (<5%) have true temporal link.
Q: BRUE คืออะไร และ low-risk vs high-risk criteria แตกต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: BRUE: Brief Resolved Unexplained Event — 2016 AAP replaces ALTE; infant <1 year with ≥1 of: cyanosis/pallor, absent/↓/irregular breathing, marked tone change, altered responsiveness — no explanation after full H+P. Low-risk (ALL criteria): >60 days age; GA ≥32 wk + PCA ≥45 wk; first BRUE; duration <1 min; no CPR; benign H+P → limited workup/observation. High-risk: does not meet all low-risk criteria → hospitalization; most BRUE patients in ED are high-risk.
Q: Apneic threshold คืออะไรและมีความสำคัญทางคลินิกอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Apneic threshold: PaCO2 level below which apnea occurs. Baseline PaCO2 ห่างจาก apneic threshold เพียง 1–1.3 mmHg (Khan 2005). Clinical importance: (1) mild hyperventilation from stimulation → CO2 drops 1 mmHg → crosses threshold → apnea; (2) hypoxemia → ↓ metabolism → ↓ CO2 production → narrows gap further; (3) caffeine → ↑ CO2 sensitivity → effectively widens buffer zone; (4) explains why even routine nursing cares can trigger apnea in very preterm infants.
Q: อธิบาย serotonin และ 5-HTT polymorphism ใน context ของ SIDS

ตอบ: 5-HT neurons in raphe nuclei: critical for central chemoreception maturation + arousal responses. 5-HTT long allele (Weese-Mayer 2003): SIDS victims in multiple racial cohorts → ↑ 5-HTT → ↑ serotonin reuptake → ↓ synaptic 5-HT → delayed maturation of serotonergic neurons in arcuate nucleus/respiratory groups → failure of arousal + inadequate response to life-threatening event. Perinatal nicotine also → ↓ chemosensitivity of 5-HT neurons (Avraam 2020) — explaining smoking as SIDS risk factor.
Q: Discharge criteria สำหรับ apnea of prematurity คืออะไร และ 7-day rule แปลว่าอะไร?

ตอบ: AAP 2016: (1) consider stopping caffeine when free of clinically significant apnea/bradycardia off pressure support for 5–7 days OR at 33–34 wk PMA; (2) brief isolated bradycardias that self-resolve + feeding events that resolve with feeding pause → generally do NOT delay discharge; (3) lower HR alarm at >33–34 wk reasonable. 7-day rule (Lorch 2011): 7-day apnea + bradycardia-free interval → 95% success rate (no recurrence) for entire cohort; risk depends on GA + PMA at last event.
Q: Prone positioning ใน NICU: ข้อดี และ เมื่อไหร่ที่ต้อง transition เป็น supine?

ตอบ: Prone in NICU: ↑ cardiorespiratory stability in extremely preterm — ↓ bradycardia + desaturation (Shepherd Sleep 2020); effect not related to PMA; ↑ FRC, ↓ work of breathing, ↓ apnea. Transition to supine: at 32 wk PMA for safe sleep conditioning before discharge. Clinical bottom line: clinically unstable preterm → prone is better for stability; clinically stable preterm approaching discharge → practice safe sleep position (supine) to condition and educate family.

Day 166 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.69
บทต่อไป: Chapter 69 — Upper Airway Lesions. เตรียมตัว: anatomy of neonatal upper airway + developmental vulnerability; congenital lesions — choanal atresia (CHARGE syndrome, bilateral emergency, neonatal resuscitation), laryngomalacia, subglottic stenosis, laryngeal webs/cysts; vascular rings + slings: double aortic arch, right aortic arch + aberrant LSCA — stridor + dysphagia; vocal cord paralysis (unilateral vs bilateral — airway emergency vs feeding difficulty); management: airway positioning, airway adjuncts (NPA, OPA), surgical timing (tracheostomy criteria); approach to neonatal stridor — differential + workup.
